title: 10 best herbal teas for shortness of breath
intro_desc: Herbal teas for shortness of breath are a natural and soothing way to alleviate symptoms of respiratory distress. These teas work by relaxing the airways, reducing inflammation, and improving lung function, making it easier to breathe. Examples include peppermint, ginger, and eucalyptus teas, which have been shown to help ease congestion and open up air passages. Drinking these teas has improved lives for those with chronic respiratory conditions such as COPD, asthma, and bronchitis, allowing them to live more comfortably and confidently.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with shortness of breath because it contains a unique blend of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, terpenes, and volatile oils. These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and relax the airways, reducing congestion and opening up passageways for easier breathing. Additionally, the tea's natural antispasmodic effects can also help calm down bronchial spasms, allowing for more efficient oxygen exchange and alleviating feelings of breathlessness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower tea's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to shortness of breath by improving respiratory function.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds found in purple coneflower tea, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, may help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Expectorant properties: Purple coneflower tea's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, reducing shortness of breath caused by bronchitis, pneumonia, or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as phenolic acids and saponins, which help to ease respiratory congestion and alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.
Seeds: Purple coneflower seeds are rich in fatty acids and other nutrients that can help to soothe and calm the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from your local herb garden or store.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea purpurea herbs with boiling water in a large tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea into a cup using a spoon or tea strainer, and discard the solids.
Drink the echinacea purpurea tea immediately while still warm, taking 2 cups per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with shortness of breath because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This property helps to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause breathing difficulties. Additionally, licorice tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. As a result, herbal licorice teas have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), providing relief for individuals suffering from shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe by decreasing swelling and congestion.
Flavonoids: Act as a natural bronchodilator, relaxing the airway muscles and increasing airflow, thereby alleviating shortness of breath.
Saponins: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the lungs from damage and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to breathing difficulties.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known as a natural expectorant, the roots help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and promoting easier breathing.
Barks: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, the barks soothe and calm irritated respiratory passages, reducing inflammation and improving airflow.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried licorice root powder or 4-6 whole licorice roots.
Boil one quart of water in a medium-sized pot for 10 minutes.
Add the licorice root powder or whole licorice roots to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes before straining and discarding the solids.
Pour the tea into a cup and let it cool for 2-3 minutes before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with shortness of breath because its potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties help to ease respiratory congestion and calm irritated airways. The tea's expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear out mucus, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, dandelion's natural diuretic effects can help to reduce fluid retention in the lungs, further alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath. Overall, drinking dandelion tea may provide natural relief from respiratory discomfort and improve overall lung function.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tea's diuretic properties can help reduce swelling and congestion in the lungs, making it easier to breathe by allowing for more efficient air exchange.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which may contribute to shortness of breath and other respiratory issues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the airways, allowing for improved airflow and reduced symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing bronchial congestion and reducing inflammation associated with shortness of breath.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that help relax airway muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve lung function, alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Rinse the herbs with cold filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 20-30 grams of dried taraxacum officinale herbs with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed teas helps with shortness of breath because it contains a compound called artemesinin, which has been shown to relax airway muscles and open up bronchial passages. This natural relaxation also reduces inflammation in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out excess mucus, further alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath. By addressing these underlying factors, Joe pye weed teas can provide relief for individuals suffering from respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Joe Pye Weed tea helps relax airway muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can contribute to shortness of breath.
Expectorant properties: The tea's expectorant compounds loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion associated with shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Joe Pye Weed tea has anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to chronic respiratory issues like bronchitis or emphysema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Joe Pye Weed roots have a calming effect on the respiratory system, making them an effective treatment for shortness of breath.
Barks: The barks of the Joe Pye Weed plant contain compounds that help to relax bronchial muscles, relieving constricted airways and reducing symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and flowers measuring 2-3 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the herb material with boiling water in a teapot or infuser allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Add honey to taste if desired mixing well before serving hot or cooling slightly before refrigerating.
Consume the prepared eupatorium perfoliatum tea twice daily as needed ideally 30 minutes prior to meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein teas helps with shortness of breath because it contains a unique combination of compounds that work together to relax and open up airways. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers in the morning or afternoon when they are dry.
Rinse the collected herbs gently with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 10 grams of dried verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers with 200 milliliters of boiling water for steeping.
Steep the herbal mixture for 15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea liquid.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ivy teas helps with shortness of breath because it contains antioxidants and flavonoids that help relax bronchial muscles, reducing inflammation in the lungs. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe and calm the airways, making it easier to breathe deeply. Bronchodilatory effects: Ivy tea has been shown to relax airway muscles, widening the bronchi and improving airflow, making it easier to breathe for individuals with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) or other respiratory conditions characterized by shortness of breath.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of hedera helix, store in airtight container for up to six months or use fresh leaves.
Measure one teaspoon of dried hedera helix leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves into a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the hedera helix leaves and let steep for five to seven minutes to release flavonoids.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding solids after three to five minutes of infusion time.
Serve the herbal tea warm or at room temperature immediately after preparation or store in refrigerator for up to two days and reheat before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with shortness of breath because it contains apigenin, an antioxidant that has a calming effect on the lungs and airways. When consumed as a tea, chamomile's soothing properties help to relax the muscles in the chest and throat, making it easier to breathe deeply and freely. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, further alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath. As a result, sipping on a warm cup of herbal chamomile tea can provide rapid relief from this common respiratory issue.
remedy_properties: Apigenin: An antioxidant flavone that has anti-inflammatory and relaxing effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate bronchospasm, thereby easing shortness of breath.

Aroma compounds: The aromatic oils present in chamomile tea, such as bisabolol and alpha-bisabolol, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can calm respiratory muscles and improve lung function, reducing feelings of shortness of breath.
Saponins: A type of glycoside that has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating shortness of breath.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried chamomile flowers in a clean glass container for later use.
Measure one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Bring filtered water to a boil and pour it over the chamomile flowers, allowing steeping for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers and pour it into a heat-resistant cup.
Heat filtered water to boiling point and pour it over the dried flowers in the cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add one tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea and stir well, if desired.
Enjoy the tea immediately while still warm, as it is best consumed within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from your garden or purchase dried ones at a reputable store.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried thyme flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water needed.
Bring the water to a boil in a large saucepan and then reduce the heat to simmering temperature for ten minutes.
Steep the thyme in the hot water for five to seven minutes, allowing the herbal essence to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving immediately.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves, using scissors to cut off top growth for maximum potency.
Rinse harvested leaves gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry immediately.
Combine one teaspoon of dried rosmarinis officinalis leaves or four teaspoons of fresh leaves with 8 ounces boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the herbal mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the hot water, before straining the liquid.
Serve the prepared tea warm or at room temperature, taking slow sips regularly throughout the day as needed.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried ginger root and 1 cup of boiling water.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea if desired.
Allow the tea to cool and steep for an additional 10-15 minutes before consumption.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves measuring one tablespoon per cup of boiling water.
Combine the leaves with one quart of cold filtered water in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a warm dark place to release active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass jar.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat shortness of breath with herbal teas is to brew a soothing cup of Thyme tea, which relaxes airways and reduces inflammation. Alternatively, Ginger tea's warming properties help ease congestion and open up the airways. Another option is Echinacea tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate respiratory issues. Drink 1-2 cups daily to experience relief from shortness of breath.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shortness of breath that are treated with herbal teas are bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Herbs such as thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint have been used to ease congestion and inflammation in the lungs. Other herbs like ginkgo biloba and hawthorn may help improve circulation and oxygenation of the body, reducing symptoms of shortness of breath.
